metadata.description: Researching New Media & Society (SAGE) journal for potential manuscript submission: scope, recent publications, submission guidelines, metrics, editorial focus, and comparison with Nature Machine Intelligence and Scientific Reports.

summary: Conducted comprehensive web research on New Media & Society (SAGE) for potential manuscript resubmission. Gathered data on: journal scope/aims, editorial leadership (Steve Jones, UIC), submission guidelines (8000 word limit, SAGE Harvard style, blind review), impact metrics (IF 8.02, H-index 160, Q1 in Communication and Sociology), recent AI-related publications (Decoding Artificial Sociality special issue Oct 2025, Automating Communication special issue 2024, multiple 2025-2026 AI articles), and comparison with Nature Machine Intelligence (IF 18.44, CS/AI focus, 3500 word limit) and Scientific Reports (IF 4.08, natural sciences focus, ~57% acceptance rate). Key finding: NM&S scope strongly aligns with the manuscript topic.

contribution.description: Comprehensive research report on New Media & Society (SAGE) journal: scope, metrics, submission guidelines, recent AI-related publications, special issues, editorial leadership, and comparison with Nature Machine Intelligence and Scientific Reports.

annotation.content: NM&S previously received a submission of this paper in 2025 (per TRACE learnings). The journal's scope (AI discourse, public debate, platform culture, creative labor) strongly aligns with the manuscript's topic of artist concerns being compressed in public AI discourse. The journal's recent special issues on 'Decoding Artificial Sociality' (Vol 27 Issue 10, Oct 2025) and 'Automating Communication in the Digital Society' (2024) demonstrate active editorial interest in AI-related social/cultural research. Word limit is 8000 words including everything.
